0_Overview: What is Kotlin: Modern, Statically Typed JVM Language by JetBrains
Benefits: 100% Interoperable with Java
Benefits: Null Safety Built into the Type System
Benefits: Concise — Eliminates Java Boilerplate
Benefits: Coroutines for Structured Concurrency
Benefits: Official Android Development Language
Benefits: Multiplatform: JVM, Android, JS, Native (Kotlin Multiplatform)
Benefits: Smart Casts, Data Classes, Extension Functions
Drawbacks: Slower Compilation than Java
Drawbacks: Larger Binary Size on Android
Drawbacks: Kotlin Multiplatform Still Maturing
Drawbacks: Some Java Interop Edge Cases
When to Use: Android App Development
When to Use: Server-side with Spring Boot or Ktor
When to Use: Replacing Java in Existing JVM Projects
When to Use: Kotlin Multiplatform for Shared Business Logic
When NOT to Use: iOS-only apps (use Swift), Performance-critical systems
Kotlin vs Java: Null Safety, Coroutines, Conciseness
Kotlin vs Swift: Android vs iOS, Similar Modern Features
Kotlin vs Dart (Flutter): JVM vs Custom VM
Kotlin Versions and Release Cadence
1_Kotlin_Basics: val vs var: Immutable vs Mutable
Null Safety: Nullable Types T?, Non-null T
let, run, with, apply, also — Scope Functions
Data Classes: equals, hashCode, toString, copy, componentN
Enum Classes with Properties and Methods
Collections: List, Set, Map (Mutable and Immutable)
2_Functions_and_OOP: Higher-order Functions: Functions as Parameters and Return Values
Delegation: by keyword — Class and Property Delegation
Lazy Delegation: val x by lazy { ... }
3_Collections_and_Functional: Collection Operations: map, filter, reduce, fold, groupBy, sortedBy
any, all, none, count, find, first, last
Sequence vs Collection: When to Use Each
4_Coroutines: Why Coroutines: Lightweight Threads, No Callback Hell
suspend Functions: Can be Paused and Resumed
runBlocking: Bridge Between Blocking and Coroutine World
SupervisorJob: Child Failures Don't Cancel Siblings
Structured Concurrency: Coroutines Tied to Scope
Flow Operators: map, filter, transform, collect, take, debounce, distinctUntilChanged
StateFlow: Hot Flow for State (replaces LiveData)
Channel: Hot Stream for Communication Between Coroutines

Activity Lifecycle: onCreate, onStart, onResume, onPause, onStop, onDestroy
LiveData vs StateFlow for UI State
Navigation Component: NavGraph, NavController, Safe Args
App Architecture: MVVM, MVI, Clean Architecture
6_Jetpack_Compose: Compose vs XML: Declarative vs Imperative UI
Layouts: Column, Row, Box, LazyColumn, LazyRow, LazyGrid
Modifiers: padding, fillMaxSize, clickable, background, clip

Shared Business Logic: Domain, Data Layers
